Title: Staff Analyst
Description:
Job Description

At Boeing, we innovate and collaborate to make the world a better place. We’re committed to fostering an environment for every teammate that’s welcoming, respectful and inclusive, with great opportunity for professional growth. Find your future with us.

Boeing Commercial Airplanes (BCA) is seeking Staff Analyst (Level 3) to join the 737 Final Assembly Staffing team in Renton, WA on 1st Shift.

We are excited to initiate this requisition to hire a Staff Analyst to join the team supporting 737 wing manufacturing at Renton. This role offers a unique opportunity to contribute directly to the heart of Boeing’s mission - building the wings products that enable flight by turning production data into actionable improvements that boost throughput, quality, and safety. The ideal candidate will bring sharp analytical skills, a continuous-improvement mindset, and a passion for solving real-world manufacturing challenges that help deliver industry-leading aircraft to customers around the world.

Primary Responsibilities:



Develop, coordinate, and assess the architecture of meeting content and leadership briefings to ensure alignment with organizational objectives
Analyze and summarize data including budget information, workforce levels, training requirements, and equipment/facility needs to support decision-making
Lead the development, planning, and execution of employee-related activities and initiatives
Partner with organizational leaders on long-range strategies, goals, and objectives
Provide continuous assessment of meeting and review structures to improve effectiveness
Develop executive-level briefings and lead the design, logistics, and facilitation of meetings and events
Conduct research and analyze data from multiple sources to support organizational performance tracking
Utilize best practices in email correspondence, meeting notices, formatting, and document management to enhance communication and efficiency
Support safety and quality initiatives
Foster a culture of first-time quality and continuous improvement.
Assist leaders and teams in completing action items by setting reminders, facilitating meetings, and providing clear instructions
Organize and maintain SharePoint and shared server content



Basic Qualifications (Required Skills/Experience):



3+ years of experience collecting data from multiple sources, performing analysis, and presenting findings
3+ years of experience utilizing Microsoft Office applications
1+ years of experience facilitating meetings and presenting to senior leaders



Preferred Qualifications (Desired Skills/Experience):



5+ years of related work experience or an equivalent combination of education and experience
1+ years of experience leading teams in a formal and /or informal leadership role
Proven ability to build positive relationships, collaborate effectively, and communicate clearly
Strong communication skills, with the ability to engage at the Senior Executive level



Conflict of Interest: Successful candidates for this job must satisfy the Company's Conflict of Interest (COI) assessment process

Drug Free Workplace:

Boeing is a Drug Free Workplace where post offer applicants and employees are subject to testing for marijuana, cocaine, opioids, amphetamines, PCP, and alcohol when criteria is met as outlined in our policies.

Pay & Benefits:

At Boeing, we strive to deliver a Total Rewards package that will attract, engage and retain the top talent. Elements of the Total Rewards package include competitive base pay and variable compensation opportunities.

The Boeing Company also provides eligible employees with an opportunity to enroll in a variety of benefit programs, generally including health insurance, flexible spending accounts, health savings accounts, retirement savings plans, life and disability insurance programs, and a number of programs that provide for both paid and unpaid time away from work.

The specific programs and options available to any given employee may vary depending on eligibility factors such as geographic location, date of hire, and the applicability of collective bargaining agreements.

Pay is based upon candidate experience and qualifications, as well as market and business considerations.

Summary Pay Range:

Level 3 - $82,000 - $93,000

Applications for this position will be accepted until Jun. 30, 2026

Export Control Requirements:

This is not an Export Control position.

Relocation

This position offers relocation based on candidate eligibility.

Visa Sponsorship

Employer will not sponsor applicants for employment visa status.

Shift

This position is for 1st shift

Equal Opportunity Employer:

Boeing is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, physical or mental disability, genetic factors, military/veteran status or other characteristics protected by law.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst – Associate or Mid-Level
Description:
Job Description

At Boeing, we innovate and collaborate to make the world a better place. We’re committed to fostering an environment for every teammate that’s welcoming, respectful and inclusive, with great opportunity for professional growth. Find your future with us.

Boeing Defense, Space and Security (BDS) Mobility, Surveillance & Bombers (MS&B) division is hiring a Business Intelligence Analyst (Level 2 or 3) as we expand our division-aligned Business Analytics team to lead and manage the development of analytical tools to increase visibility of program performance and support data-driven decision making. In joining this team, you will have a critical part in the division's analytical transformation including program metric identification and development, KPI standardization and management, and descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics development and deployment. This role will be based in Tukwila, WA.

Strong communication and organizational skills are critical to success in this position, along with the ability to build strong relationships, collaborate in a cross-functional environment, and utilize problem-solving skills to proactively identify and manage risks and opportunities.

Position Responsibilities:



Designs and develops Business Intelligence information models, dashboards, metrics and reports utilizing standard technologies and methodologies.
Combines programming, SQL skills, best practices, strategic decision-making capabilities and business expertise to deliver efficient and scalable Business Intelligence solutions to improve engineering processes, engineering performance, business productivity and business outcomes.
Data Preprocessing and Cleaning: Ensure the quality and accuracy of large volumes of structured and unstructured data using SQL or other coding languages.
Develop ETL pipelines, ensuring high-quality data capture.
Supports establishment of strategic direction of descriptive analytics for the division and functional partners, including identifying opportunities for analytical solution development
Identify key performance indicators (KPIs) to provide accurate visibility of program and functional performance.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and business objectives.
Partner with stakeholders and internal customer to ensure follow up action for issue resolution.
Clearly articulate technical concepts and information to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Effectively train individuals on how to utilize analytical tools, ensuring clear understanding of the information



This position is expected to be 100% onsite. The selected candidate will be required to work onsite at one of the listed location options.

This position requires the ability to obtain a U.S. Security Clearance for which the U.S. Government requires U.S. Citizenship. An interim and/or final U.S. Top Secret Clearance Post-Start is required.

Basic Qualifications (Required Skills/Experience): Level 2



3+ years of experience in identifying and communicating business requirements with cross-functional and/or cross-business teams
1+ year of experience with database technologies using SQL or PL/SQL
1+ year of experience in designing/building visualizations within Tableau
Experience with building ETL pipelines
1+ year of experience in data analysis algorithms (e.g. data mining, statistics, machine learning, natural language processing, text mining, visual analytics) and building Descriptive, Predictive and Prescriptive models
1+ year of experience developing and maintaining relationships and partnerships with stakeholders (i.e. customers, suppliers, peers and/or partners)
1+ year of experience working with engineering data



Basic Qualifications (Required Skills/Experience): Level 3



5+ years of experience in identifying and communicating business requirements with cross-functional and/or cross-business teams
3+ years of experience with database technologies using SQL or PL/SQL
3+ years of experience in designing/building visualizations within Tableau
Experience with building ETL pipelines
3+ years of experience in data analysis algorithms (e.g. data mining, statistics, machine learning, natural language processing, text mining, visual analytics) and building Descriptive, Predictive and Prescriptive models
3+ years of experience developing and maintaining relationships and partnerships with stakeholders (i.e. customers, suppliers, peers and/or partners)
1+ year of experience working with engineering data



Preferred Qualifications (Desired Skills/Experience):



Master’s degree or higher, study in a technical field (i.e. Computer Science, Information Management, etc.)
Understanding of aerospace engineering processes and data
Experience with Palantir Foundry
Experience with R or Python data visualization packages (i.e. Shiny, Dash, Streamlit)
Strong Cloud background, specifically in Azure and Azure Managed Services
Experience in Dev Ops: CI/CD pipelines
Level 2: 1 or more years' related work experience or an equivalent combination of education and experience
Level 3: 3 or more years' related work experience or an equivalent combination of education and experience



Relocation assistance is not a negotiable benefit for this position. Candidates must live in the immediate area or relocate at their own expense.

Travel: The ability and willingness to travel domestically up to 15% of the time

Shift: 1st Shift

Drug Free Workplace:

Boeing is a Drug Free Workplace where post offer applicants and employees are subject to testing for marijuana, cocaine, opioids, amphetamines, PCP, and alcohol when criteria is met as outlined in our policies.

Total Rewards & Pay Transparency:

At Boeing, we strive to deliver a Total Rewards package that will attract, engage and retain the top talent. Elements of the Total Rewards package include competitive base pay and variable compensation opportunities.

The Boeing Company also provides eligible employees with an opportunity to enroll in a variety of benefit programs, generally including health insurance, flexible spending accounts, health savings accounts, retirement savings plans, life and disability insurance programs, and a number of programs that provide for both paid and unpaid time away from work.

The specific programs and options available to any given employee may vary depending on eligibility factors such as geographic location, date of hire, and the applicability of collective bargaining agreements.

The Boeing 401(k) helps you save for your future, with contributions from Boeing that can help you grow your retirement savings. Our best-in-class retirement benefit features:



Best in class 401(k) plan: we'll match your contributions dollar for dollar, up to 10% of eligible pay with immediate 100% vesting
Student Loan Match: The Boeing 401(k) Student Loan Match allows eligible enrolled U.S. employees to have their qualified student loan debt payments counted, along with any match-eligible contributions they make, for purposes of determining the Company Match to employees' Boeing 401(k) accounts.



Pay is based upon candidate experience and qualifications, as well as market and business considerations.

Summary pay range:

Level 2: $81,600 - $110,400

Level 3: $102,850 - $139,150

Applications for this position will be accepted until June 28, 2026

Applications for this position will be accepted until Jun. 29, 2026

Export Control Requirements:

This position must meet U.S. export control compliance requirements. To meet U.S. export control compliance requirements, a “U.S. Person” as defined by 22 C.F.R.



120.62 is required. “U.S. Person” includes U.S. Citizen, U.S. National, lawful permanent resident, refugee, or asylee.



Export Control Details:

US based job, US Person required

Relocation

Relocation assistance is not a negotiable benefit for this position.

Security Clearance

This position requires the ability to obtain a U.S. Security Clearance for which the U.S. Government requires U.S. Citizenship. An interim and/or final U.S. Top Secret Clearance Post-Start is required.

Visa Sponsorship

Employer will not sponsor applicants for employment visa status.

Shift

This position is for 1st shift

Title: Data Engineer, Product Analytics
Description:
As a Data Engineer at Meta, you will shape the future of people-facing and business-facing products we build across our entire family of applications (Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, WhatsApp, Reality Labs, Threads). Your technical skills and analytical mindset will be utilized designing and building some of the world's most extensive data sets, helping to craft experiences for billions of people and hundreds of millions of businesses worldwide.In this role, you will collaborate with software engineering, data science, and product management teams to design/build scalable data solutions across Meta to optimize growth, strategy, and user experience for our 3 billion plus users, as well as our internal employee community.You will be at the forefront of identifying and solving some of the most interesting data challenges at a scale few companies can match. By joining Meta, you will become part of a world-class data engineering community dedicated to skill development and career growth in data engineering and beyond.Data Engineering: You will guide teams by building optimal data artifacts (including datasets and visualizations) to address key questions. You will refine our systems, design logging solutions, and create scalable data models. Ensuring data security and quality, and with a strong focus on efficiency, you will suggest architecture and development approaches and data management standards to address complex analytical problems.Product leadership: You will use data to shape product development, identify new opportunities, and tackle upcoming challenges. You'll ensure our products add value for users and businesses, by prioritizing projects, and driving innovative solutions to respond to challenges or opportunities.Communication and influence: You won't simply present data, but tell data-driven stories. You will convince and influence your partners using clear insights and recommendations. You will build credibility through structure and clarity, and be a trusted strategic partner.

Data Engineer, Product Analytics Responsibilities:


Conceptualize and own the data architecture for multiple large-scale projects, while evaluating design and operational cost-benefit tradeoffs within systems
Create and contribute to frameworks that improve the efficacy of logging data, while working with data infrastructure to triage issues and resolve
Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and data scientists to understand data needs, representing key data insights in a meaningful way
Define and manage Service Level Agreements for all data sets in allocated areas of ownership
Determine and implement the security model based on privacy requirements, confirm safeguards are followed, address data quality issues, and evolve governance processes within allocated areas of ownership
Design, build, and launch collections of sophisticated data models and visualizations that support multiple use cases across different products or domains
Solve our most challenging data integration problems, utilizing optimal Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) patterns, frameworks, query techniques, sourcing from structured and unstructured data sources
Assist in owning existing processes running in production, optimizing complex code through advanced algorithmic concepts
Optimize pipelines, dashboards, frameworks, and systems to facilitate easier development of data artifacts
Influence product and cross-functional teams to identify data opportunities to drive impact
Mentor team members by giving/receiving actionable feedback


Minimum Qualifications:


4+ years of experience with SQL, ETL, data modeling, and at least one programming language (e.g., Python, C++, C#, Scala, etc.)


Preferred Qualifications:


Master's or Ph.D degree in a STEM field
Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements)
Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews)
Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies


About Meta:

Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

Meta is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Meta participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law. Please note that Meta may leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies in connection with applications for employment.

Meta is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities in our recruiting process. If you need any assistance or accommodations due to a disability, please let us know at accommodations-ext@meta.com.

$147,000/year to $208,000/year + bonus + equity + benefits

Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

Title: Data Engineer, Product Analytics
Description:
As a Data Engineer at Meta, you will shape the future of people-facing and business-facing products we build across our entire family of applications (Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, WhatsApp, Reality Labs, Threads). Your technical skills and analytical mindset will be utilized designing and building some of the world's most extensive data sets, helping to craft experiences for billions of people and hundreds of millions of businesses worldwide.In this role, you will collaborate with software engineering, data science, and product management teams to design/build scalable data solutions across Meta to optimize growth, strategy, and user experience for our 3 billion plus users, as well as our internal employee community.You will be at the forefront of identifying and solving some of the most interesting data challenges at a scale few companies can match. By joining Meta, you will become part of a world-class data engineering community dedicated to skill development and career growth in data engineering and beyond.Data Engineering: You will guide teams by building optimal data artifacts (including datasets and visualizations) to address key questions. You will refine our systems, design logging solutions, and create scalable data models. Ensuring data security and quality, and with a strong focus on efficiency, you will suggest architecture and development approaches and data management standards to address complex analytical problems.Product leadership: You will use data to shape product development, identify new opportunities, and tackle upcoming challenges. You'll ensure our products add value for users and businesses, by prioritizing projects, and driving innovative solutions to respond to challenges or opportunities.Communication and influence: You won't simply present data, but tell data-driven stories. You will convince and influence your partners using clear insights and recommendations. You will build credibility through structure and clarity, and be a trusted strategic partner.

Data Engineer, Product Analytics Responsibilities:


Conceptualize and own the data architecture for multiple large-scale projects, while evaluating design and operational cost-benefit tradeoffs within systems
Create and contribute to frameworks that improve the efficacy of logging data, while working with data infrastructure to triage issues and resolve
Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and data scientists to understand data needs, representing key data insights in a meaningful way
Define and manage Service Level Agreements for all data sets in allocated areas of ownership
Determine and implement the security model based on privacy requirements, confirm safeguards are followed, address data quality issues, and evolve governance processes within allocated areas of ownership
Design, build, and launch collections of sophisticated data models and visualizations that support multiple use cases across different products or domains
Solve our most challenging data integration problems, utilizing optimal Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) patterns, frameworks, query techniques, sourcing from structured and unstructured data sources
Assist in owning existing processes running in production, optimizing complex code through advanced algorithmic concepts
Optimize pipelines, dashboards, frameworks, and systems to facilitate easier development of data artifacts
Influence product and cross-functional teams to identify data opportunities to drive impact
Mentor team members by giving/receiving actionable feedback


Minimum Qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience
4+ years of experience where the primary responsibility involves working with data. This could include roles such as data analyst, data scientist, data engineer, or similar positions
4+ years of experience with SQL, ETL, data modeling, and at least one programming language (e.g., Python, C++, C#, Scala, etc.)


Preferred Qualifications:


Master's or Ph.D degree in a STEM field
Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements)
Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews)
Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies


About Meta:

Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

Meta is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Meta participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law. Please note that Meta may leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies in connection with applications for employment.

Meta is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities in our recruiting process. If you need any assistance or accommodations due to a disability, please let us know at accommodations-ext@meta.com.

$147,000/year to $208,000/year + bonus + equity + benefits

Title: Senior Strategist, Ads and Shopping, Scams
Description:
In accordance with Washington state law, we are highlighting our comprehensive benefits package, which is available to all eligible US based employees. Benefits for this role include:


Health, dental, vision, life, disability insurance
Retirement Benefits: 401(k) with company match
Paid Time Off: 20 days of vacation per year, accruing at a rate of 6.15 hours per pay period for the first five years of employment
Sick Time: 40 hours/year (increased to 69 hours/year for Seattle) including 5 discretionary sick days per instance
Maternity Leave (Short-Term Disability + Baby Bonding): 28-30 weeks
Baby Bonding Leave: 18 weeks
Holidays: 13 paid days per year


Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
4 years of experience in data analytics, trust and safety, policy, cybersecurity, or related fields.


Preferred qualifications:


Master's degree.
Education in, or experience with, machine learning.
Experience in SQL, building dashboards, data collection/transformation, visualization/dashboards, or experience in a scripting/programming language (e.g., Python).
Excellent communication and presentation skills (written and verbal) and the ability to influence cross-functionally at various levels.


About the jobTrust and Safety team members are tasked with identifying and taking on the biggest problems that challenge the safety and integrity of our products. They use technical know-how, excellent problem-solving skills, user insights, and proactive communication to protect users and our partners from abuse across Google products like Search, Maps, Gmail, and Google Ads. On this team, you're a big-picture thinker and strategic team-player with a passion for doing what’s right. You work globally and cross-functionally with Google engineers and product managers to identify and fight abuse and fraud cases at Google speed - with urgency. And you take pride in knowing that every day you are working hard to promote trust in Google and ensuring the highest levels of user safety.

The Trust and Safety (T&S) Ads and Shopping - Scams team is leading the way in reducing scams while improving user experience on the platform. Our goal is integral to our day-to-day work. As a team, we work across multiple policy areas and focus on using various solutions to address the ever changing scam landscape.

As a Trust and Safety Ads Scams Analyst, you will be responsible for protecting users from offline scams - scams which take place outside the Google Ads Platform. You will use data to provide recommendations to products and devise solutions (global and hyperlocal) to stay ahead of the fraudsters. You will also be responsible for ensuring good advertisers are provided the right opportunity to serve. You will use Applied AI, machine learning or other techniques to optimize human review cost and efficiency while continuously identifying enforcement gaps and automation opportunities.

At Google we work hard to earn our users’ trust every day. Trust and Safety is Google’s team of abuse fighting and user trust experts working daily to make the internet a safer place. We partner with teams across Google to deliver bold solutions in abuse areas such as malware, spam and account hijacking. A team of Analysts, Policy Specialists, Engineers, and Program Managers, we work to reduce risk and fight abuse across all of Google’s products, protecting our users, advertisers, and publishers across the globe in over 40 languages.Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

US: $116000 - $167000 (USD) + 15% bonus target + equity + benefits

Responsibilities

Learn more about benefits at Google .


Collaborate with cross-functional groups such as PM, Engineering, Sales and Legal to drive projects that increases users' safety and provide better online experience through Google Ads.
Execute complex queries and analyses, build visual models, and construct presentations on abuse trends to aid in fraud prevention and influence change in existing abuse detection systems without compromising the advertiser experience.
Conduct investigations to catch fraudsters, enforce our product policies, learn scam patterns/trends and identify product vulnerabilities.
Identify automation and efficiency opportunities and drive solutions through analysis or cross-functional partnerships.
Perform on-call responsibilities on a rotating basis, including weekend coverage/holidays. This role works with sensitive content or situations and may be exposed to graphic, controversial, or upsetting topics or content.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

Title: Document Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Document Reviewer

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.

Key Responsibilities


Carefully review large volumes of structured and unstructured data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Identify and flag issues related to data quality, consistency, and potential risks involving Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Annotate, classify, and, where necessary, redact sensitive information to maintain privacy and compliance standards.
Follow strict guidelines, taxonomies, and workflow processes for data categorization and validation.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks to verify data integrity and validate outputs.
Maintain detailed records of reviewed documents and actions taken to ensure traceability and accountability.
Collaborate with the customer's team, providing clear and concise written and verbal communication about findings and recommendations.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience in document review, auditing, annotation, redaction, or data classification at scale.
Exceptional attention to detail with a high level of accuracy in repetitive tasks.
Familiarity with handling and protecting Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to document findings and communicate effectively with team members.
Demonstrated ability to follow complex guidelines and adhere to established taxonomies.
Experience performing QA or validation work on large data sets.
Ability to balance efficiency with precision in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in legal, compliance, privacy, auditing, compliance or information security domains.
Prior experience supporting data-driven AI or machine learning projects.
Comfort with collaboration tools and digital workflow management platforms.

Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Kirkland, WA, USA; Atlanta, GA, USA; Austin, TX, USA; Chicago, IL, USA; Reston, VA, USA.Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
7 years of experience in program management, sales operations, or management consulting.
Experience analyzing data and creating reports with spreadsheets, utilizing database queries, or performing quantitative modeling and forecasting.
Experience managing operations or programs in a global role.

Master's degree in Financial Engineering, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
Experience with database queries (e.g. SQL), data visualization tools (e.g. Tableau) and building dashboards.
Experience designing and building an organizational wide data repository strategy and the corresponding business intelligence infrastructure, in a finance organization.
Ability to drive complex operational initiatives with a strategic orientation.
Excellent business partnering and judgment skills, with the ability to work in a cross-functional, fluid environment.


About The Job

The Cloud Partner Incentives and Investments Strategy and Operations team is focused on accelerating the adoption of Google Cloud by attracting and growing partners who can acquire customers, grow existing customers, and innovate to grow their success.

As a Global Partner Incentives Design Lead, you will be responsible for driving the strategy, design, development and launch of partner incentives for the multi-billion Google Cloud portfolio. You will be responsible for working cross-functionally to develop and implement the strategy, planning, design and launch of partner incentives required by regions and Cloud Sales Leadership. You will closely work with key stakeholders across the organization to plan and design compelling incentives to meet our business goals.

Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.

Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

US: $164000 - $240000 (USD) + 20% bonus target + equity + benefits

Responsibilities

Learn more about benefits at Google .


Build dashboards, develop models and maintain data sets to analyze, report and present insights related to the performance of our Incentive programs.
Provide insights through reactive and proactive analysis to drive greater efficiency and effectiveness of our Incentive Programs and investment portfolio. Identify, lead and execute process improvements to reporting, understand trends and identify potential risks and opportunities.
Design, build, and maintain automated reporting, dashboards, and analyses to enable our team self-service data needs, and enable faster decision making as well as execution.
Dive into data sets to answer business questions using SQL and other data manipulation languages.
Develop, implement, and track metrics to measure incentive program performance and effectiveness. Develop digestible performance updates to senior leaders, and lead ongoing business review meetings alongside other stakeholders.

Title: BI Analyst II
Description:
Purpose Brands, the parent company of Orangetheory, Anytime Fitness, Waxing the City, and The Bar Method, is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst II, to join its team. This is a great position for someone who is looking to expand their career, and join a company with a fun, fast-paced and inspirational culture.

As part of our Analytics team, this data and analytics professional analyzes business questions, assesses the impact of business initiatives. They think critically, are mathematically savvy, know how to query data, and apply analytical methods to derive insights. They work with our data engineers to augment datasets to enable dashboarding, reports, and operationalizing insights. They partner with business stakeholders to help them make use of analysis results and deploy data-informed decision-making processes.

Purpose/Impact: (Duties & Essential Functions)

Reporting & Analytics


Analyze business questions and communicate findings through briefs, presentations, reports or other media. Clarify and document the questions, how findings may be used, and the analysis approach.
Conduct impact analyses, leading to a deeper understanding of business performance, the member experience and fitness results. Guidance on approach provided by senior analyst or BI Manager.
Develop simple deterministic models that project outcomes based on observed relationships, e.g., break-even projections, simple systems models.
Develop reports and dashboards for an entire problem space, such as website performance, lead conversion or studio portfolio analysis. These are product features that are supported and evolved over time as opposed to one-off analyses.
Develop internal and external documentation and training on Analytics products, such as dashboards, reports, KPI's, source data collection and transformation, data lineage and suggested use cases.
Develop end-user applications with low-code tools to surface actionable analytics insights, collect data or execute analytics driven workflows/actions.
Work with data engineers to augment existing datasets with metrics and categorizations that make it easier to answer common business and analysis questions and publish appropriate reports.
Build the company knowledge base of data-informed insights, documenting findings and promoting and communicating those findings through appropriate internal channels.


Performance Expectations


Able to develop or collaborate with a data engineer on the design (metrics, attributes) of “last mile” datasets for analyzing common questions in a specific business domain.
Well versed in most of the functionality our BI platforms offer and able to make efficient use of them.
Uses a toolkit of analytic methods, e.g., variance analysis, sampling, establishing correlations, methods of analyzing causality to answer business questions with data.
Able to ask questions to fully understand the context and business question asked and how answering it will make a difference. Occasionally still needs help from senior analyst or manager to frame the problem.
Chooses or proposes a path of inquiry that is logical and coherent.
Follows applicable COE (Center of Excellence) tenets and methodologies when analyzing business questions or developing reports and dashboards.
Makes accurate inferences from the data most of the time. Still needs some help from more senior analysts or manager to validate conclusions.
Presents findings in an easy-to-understand way for business stakeholders, using COE practices.
Shares and documents approach and findings, making sure the rest of the team has visibility and can build on them.
Is recognized by business stakeholders as contributing to their decision-making processes or improving their operations.
Regularly reviews work with more senior analysts, seeks to learn and incorporates learnings into work products.


Background and Strengths


BS/BA in computer science, statistics, mathematics, business administration, economics, or related field with a focus on logic and quantitative analysis.
3-5 years of experience in reporting and analysis. Experience with implementing, maintaining or developing reporting in Power BI, Tableau or comparable platforms is a plus.
Basic understanding of statistics (sampling, statistical significance).
Proficiency in querying and manipulating data using SQL. Know how to use subqueries, window and analytic functions, regular expressions.
Experience with Python, R or similar languages.


Strengths


Well organized. Thinks about how to do the work, then acts. Seeks to contextualize the work.
Demonstrates understanding of which analytic methods to use to answer certain types of questions.
Good interpersonal skills, seeks to understand others’ viewpoints, motivations, ways of thinking.
Communicates clearly and knows best practices for presentations.
Shows curiosity about the industry and can explain how our brands are differentiated.


Salary Range: $90k - $115k

What’s in it for you?

We offer a competitive salary along with exceptional benefits such as:


Medical, Dental and Vision Coverage
Hybrid Work Environment
Life and Disability Insurance
Unlimited Time off + Paid Holidays
Flexible Friday's between Memorial Day and Labor Day
401(K) Savings Plan Matching at 4%
10 Coaching and Therapy sessions
Mental Health Benefits
Brand Discounts & Reimbursements
In-house workout facilities
Professional Development Opportunities
Team Building, Employee Engagement Activities & so much more

Title: Senior Analyst, Recruiting Data Analytics
Description:
To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.

Job Category

Data

Job Details

About Salesforce

Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.

Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.

The Recruiting Analytics Insights & Research (RAIR) team is seeking a detail-oriented, mid-career Analyst. As part of the Recruiting Organization and the larger people analytics umbrella at Salesforce, we act as strategic consultants for the Recruiting Organization, providing informed analysis and business acumen that challenge myths and status quo mindsets. We leverage Salesforce’s cutting-edge technology stack, embracing the evolution of insight delivery beyond traditional BI and analytics consulting at scale. The RAIR team engages with stakeholders during critical moments, understanding their business needs and offering strategic, data-informed solutions with actionable recommendations. This role will support and evolve some of our most critical and highly visible self-service dashboards as we develop agentic and conversational analytics products for Salesforce’s next generation of Workforce Planning and Recruiting Platforms.

Responsibilities


- Predictive Modeling: Build, validate, and deploy predictive analytics models to forecast hiring trends, attrition risks, and talent supply gaps.
- ML Integration: Apply machine learning techniques (such as regression, classification, or natural language processing) to uncover deeper insights within recruiting and workforce planning datasets.
- Data-to-Insight Pipeline: Translate complex ML model outputs into actionable, strategic recommendations and clear data stories for recruiting leadership.
- Dashboard Evolution: Maintain and manage key Tableau dashboards, integrating predictive insights and advanced data models into user-friendly visualization tools.
- Process Automation: Automate manual reporting workflows and pipeline tasks to scale insight delivery across the organization.
- Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Finance, Workforce Planning, IT, and Data Engineering to align data pipelines and scale the infrastructure required for advanced analytics.
- Adapt quickly to changing priorities to ensure timely delivery in a dynamic environment..


Requirements For Success


Experience: 4+ years of professional experience in data analytics, people analytics, or recruiting analytics, with a proven track record of delivering predictive insights.
Predictive Analytics & ML: Strong foundational knowledge of machine learning concepts and predictive modeling methodologies applied to business or HR challenges.


Technical Stack


Python or R: Proficient in using Python or R for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and machine learning libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, pandas).
SQL: Strong intermediate SQL skills for querying, data preparation, and pipeline optimization.
Tableau: Proven experience designing and maintaining complex dashboards.
Agility & Drive: Ability to adapt quickly to changing priorities in a dynamic environment, with a strong curiosity to continuous upskilling in AI, agentic tools, and emerging technical languages.


Unleash Your Potential

When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and be your best, and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can do your best. Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.

Accommodations

If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form.

Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.

Posting Statement

Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

In the United States, compensation offered will be determined by factors such as location, job level, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Certain roles may be eligible for incentive compensation, equity, and benefits. Salesforce offers a variety of benefits to help you live well including: time off programs, medical, dental, vision, mental health support, paid parental leave, life and disability insurance, 401(k), and an employee stock purchasing program. More details about company benefits can be found at the following link: https://www.salesforcebenefits.com.Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and the Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring, Salesforce will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

At Salesforce, we believe in equitable compensation practices that reflect the dynamic nature of labor markets across various regions. The typical base salary range for this position is $99,400 - $150,300 annually. In select cities within the San Francisco and New York City metropolitan area, the base salary range for this role is $118,900 - $163,500 annually. The range represents base salary only, and does not include company bonus, incentive for sales roles, equity or benefits, as applicable.

Title: Data Engineer, Product Analytics
Description:
As a Data Engineer at Meta, you will shape the future of people-facing and business-facing products we build across our entire family of applications (Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, WhatsApp, Reality Labs, Threads). Your technical skills and analytical mindset will be utilized designing and building some of the world's most extensive data sets, helping to craft experiences for billions of people and hundreds of millions of businesses worldwide.In this role, you will collaborate with software engineering, data science, and product management teams to design/build scalable data solutions across Meta to optimize growth, strategy, and user experience for our 3 billion plus users, as well as our internal employee community.You will be at the forefront of identifying and solving some of the most interesting data challenges at a scale few companies can match. By joining Meta, you will become part of a world-class data engineering community dedicated to skill development and career growth in data engineering and beyond.Data Engineering: You will guide teams by building optimal data artifacts (including datasets and visualizations) to address key questions. You will refine our systems, design logging solutions, and create scalable data models. Ensuring data security and quality, and with a strong focus on efficiency, you will suggest architecture and development approaches and data management standards to address complex analytical problems.Product leadership: You will use data to shape product development, identify new opportunities, and tackle upcoming challenges. You'll ensure our products add value for users and businesses, by prioritizing projects, and driving innovative solutions to respond to challenges or opportunities.Communication and influence: You won't simply present data, but tell data-driven stories. You will convince and influence your partners using clear insights and recommendations. You will build credibility through structure and clarity, and be a trusted strategic partner.

Data Engineer, Product Analytics Responsibilities:


Conceptualize and own the data architecture for multiple large-scale projects, while evaluating design and operational cost-benefit tradeoffs within systems
Create and contribute to frameworks that improve the efficacy of logging data, while working with data infrastructure to triage issues and resolve
Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and data scientists to understand data needs, representing key data insights visually in a meaningful way
Define and manage Service Level Agreements for all data sets in allocated areas of ownership
Determine and implement the security model based on privacy requirements, confirm safeguards are followed, address data quality issues, and evolve governance processes within allocated areas of ownership
Design, build, and launch collections of sophisticated data models and visualizations that support multiple use cases across different products or domains
Solve our most challenging data integration problems, utilizing optimal Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) patterns, frameworks, query techniques, sourcing from structured and unstructured data sources
Assist in owning existing processes running in production, optimizing complex code through advanced algorithmic concepts
Optimize pipelines, dashboards, frameworks, and systems to facilitate easier development of data artifacts
Influence product and cross-functional teams to identify data opportunities to drive impact
Mentor team members by giving/receiving actionable feedback


Minimum Qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience
7+ years of experience where the primary responsibility involves working with data. This could include roles such as data analyst, data scientist, data engineer, or similar positions
7+ years of experience with SQL, ETL, data modeling, and at least one programming language (e.g., Python, C++, C#, Scala or others.)


Preferred Qualifications:


Master's or Ph.D degree in a STEM field
Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements)
Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews)
Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies


About Meta:

Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

Meta is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Meta participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law. Please note that Meta may leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies in connection with applications for employment.

Meta is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities in our recruiting process. If you need any assistance or accommodations due to a disability, please let us know at accommodations-ext@meta.com.

$177,000/year to $247,000/year + bonus + equity + benefits

Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

Title: Compliance Analyst
Description:
Job Title: iGRC Data & Compliance Analyst

Location: Bellevue, WA

Duration: 04 Months

Pay Rate: $48.00 -$52.00/hr on W2




Job Description:

Overview

We are seeking a highly detail-oriented analyst or PM to support the Enterprise Compliance organization in the implementation of an integrated Governance, Risk, and Compliance (iGRC) solution within ServiceNow (SNOW). This role is primarily focused on data entry, data analysis, quality assurance, and fact-checking activities within the iGRC platform. The ideal candidate brings hands-on experience with iGRC implementations and thrives in a structured, process-driven environment that demands accuracy and thoroughness.

Day-to-day, this contractor will report to the Compliance Manager and work closely with control testers and members of the iGRC implementation team.




Key Responsibilities

Data Entry & Management

Accurately enter, update, and maintain iGRC data within ServiceNow, including controls, risks, policies, and related records
Ensure data integrity and consistency across all iGRC modules in SNOW
Follow established data governance standards and naming conventions throughout all entries




Data Analysis & Fact-Checking

Analyze iGRC data to identify gaps, inconsistencies, or anomalies and escalate findings to the Compliance Manager
Fact-check control descriptions, risk ratings, policy mappings, and other iGRC content against source documentation and authoritative references
Cross-reference data across multiple sources to validate accuracy prior to finalization




Quality Assurance & Compliance Checks

Conduct structured quality checks on iGRC records to ensure completeness, accuracy, and alignment with compliance standards
Track and document QA findings, flag discrepancies, and follow up with relevant stakeholders to drive resolution
Support review cycles and approval workflows within SNOW, ensuring records meet defined quality thresholds before progressing




Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner with control testers to ensure testing evidence and results are accurately reflected in SNOW
Collaborate with iGRC implementation team members on data migration, configuration validation, and content population activities
Communicate status updates, blockers, and quality findings to the Compliance Manager on a daily basis
Participate in team meetings and working sessions; document action items and follow up on open items




Required Qualifications:

2+ years of hands-on experience with iGRC implementations or GRC platform administration (ServiceNow GRC/IRM strongly preferred)
Demonstrated experience with data entry, data validation, and quality control activities within a compliance or risk management context
Strong analytical skills with a meticulous attention to detail and a low tolerance for data inaccuracies
Familiarity with GRC concepts including controls, risks, policies, and compliance frameworks (e.g., SOX, NIST, ISO 27001)
Ability to work cross-functionally with technical and non-technical stakeholders
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Teams); experience using Excel for data analysis and reconciliation
Strong written and verbal communication skills; able to concisely document findings and escalate issues appropriately




Key Skills

Exceptional attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Structured, methodical approach to quality assurance and fact-checking
Ability to manage high volumes of data entry without sacrificing quality
Strong organizational skills with the ability to prioritize and track multiple workstreams simultaneously
Proactive communication — surfaces issues quickly and clearly
Comfortable working within defined processes and escalating when deviations arise




Working Arrangements

This is a contract role supporting an active iGRC implementation program.
The contractor will report daily to the Compliance Manager and is expected to maintain consistent availability aligned with the core team's working hours.
Work will be performed within ServiceNow and supporting collaboration tools (Microsoft Teams, Outlook, SharePoint).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

McLean, VA

ONSITE 5 days a week

LONG TERM CONTRACT....no tenure

2 rounds of interviews (the first virtual/phone and the final onsite)




Must have experience in data analysis, advanced SQL, and Python. Experience extracting and analyzing data, building and maintaining data models and working with various data structures and database technologies. Strong communication skills are required and must be able to effectively collaborate with internal stakeholders at all levels. Experience with Snowflake and Data bricks is a plus.




Experience:

7+ years of experience functioning as a true Data Analyst.

Project Management: Foundational project management skills are highly valued. The candidate must be structured, disciplined, and strictly follow a project schedule.

Agile and Tools Note: No Agile user story writing required. Experience with Atlassian tools (Jira/Confluence) is not necessary for this role.

Technical Stack and Tooling

SQL (Must-Have): Advanced capability; must be able to confidently write complex queries from scratch to gather and pull data from enterprise data stores.

Python: Used specifically for data processing and analysis.

Snowflake: Required familiarity to navigate the data ecosystem.

Collibra: Will be utilized heavily for data governance, metadata management, and cataloging.

Data Modeling: Preferred, but with a caveat—the candidate will not be building data models from scratch (models are already built in the schema), but they must understand them to validate and register data against them.

Title: SpendVue Data Analyst
Description:
Position Overview

The SpendVue Data Analyst plays a critical role in processing and wrangling large data sets for clients, consultants, and internal teams across the ERA Group.




In this role, the analyst will support project delivery by managing large data sets, creating dashboard reports, and ensuring that information is processed and shared in a timely and accurate manner. They will collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to troubleshoot issues, improve data quality, and provide actionable insights that support growth.




The analyst will work closely with ERA Group leadership team to monitor data quality, identify opportunities, and implement solutions that drive growth.




The SpendVue Data Analyst reports directly to the SpendVue Manager within the ERA Operations Team.




Responsibilities

Process large data sets to create a SpendVue Report (Client spend dashboards) for consultants
Data cleansing and transformation process of the SpendVue Template (Excel, 100K+ rows)
Process data through SpendBot (AI) and review accuracy for consultants
Filter and categorize cost saving categories in SpendVue Report (Excel)
Analyze and manipulate data in Excel and provide analytical insights (Pivot Tables, XLOOKUP, etc.)
Support process improvement of data processing to increase speed and accuracy of delivery
Work alongside the Global SpendVue team to create next-gen Spend Analysis / Intelligence reports (AI)
Support Franchise Business Units and Prospective Customers in uploading data for analysis
Train consultants on our data processes, and answer questions as needed
Identify and analyze business development opportunities within the Athena CRM platform
Create executive management reports using Excel, Power BI, and other business intelligence tools
Other duties and responsibilities as assigned




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Data Analytics, Information Systems, Finance, or a related field preferred. Equivalent experience, associate degrees, or relevant certifications will also be considered.
1 - 2 years of data quality or data management experience
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including Pivot Tables, XLOOKUP, and large dataset management
Microsoft Office Suite experience: Word, PowerPoint, Power BI
Tableau experience a plus
Python experience a plus
Project Management experience
Strong attention to detail and the ability to prioritize effectively
Excellent communication skills, written and verbal
Travel required at times (less than 10%)




Work Environment

Hybrid work environment with a combination of remote work and in-office collaboration in Lehi, Utah (Kiln Membership)




Why Join Us?

Competitive Salary with paid time off, paid holidays, and flexible work-from-home days
Health, dental, vision, optional life insurance, and 401k program
Fun team events and the opportunity to engage with colleagues in the UK, APAC, etc.
Growing company with development opportunities




About the ERA Group:

ERA Group, established in 1992, is a world leader in Cost Intelligence services. We optimize businesses costs and find innovative solutions to meet a company’s needs. More on ERAGroup.com

Ability to work independently in a remote setting, make informed decisions, and manage multiple projects simultaneously.

Title: Social Media Strategist
Description:
About Us

Beast Industries is a multifaceted media and entertainment company founded by Jimmy Donaldson, popularly known as MrBeast, the most watched person in the world. Renowned for revolutionizing digital content creation, Beast Industries encompasses a diverse portfolio of ventures that extend far beyond its origins on YouTube. With a mission to entertain, inspire, and create significant social impact, Beast Industries operates across various domains including digital media, philanthropy, consumer products, and innovative business initiatives. At Beast Industries, we believe in the transformative power of digital media and its potential to entertain, educate, and effect positive change. Our commitment to innovation, creativity, and philanthropy drives us to explore new frontiers, create unforgettable experiences, and build a legacy that inspires future generations.

About The Role

The Social Media Strategist is the strategic role for social media operations across Beast Industries’ CPG brands, including Feastables, MrBeast Apparel, MrBeast Labs, Lunchly, and future Beast-owned properties. This role supports the strategy that determines how each brand shows up, stays ahead of culture, and ensures the right ideas make it to production.

You’re the filter between raw creative energy and strategic execution. You work closely with the creative team to pressure-test ideas, bring trend intelligence and competitive context to the table, and drive the thoughtfulness behind every concept before it reaches the Head of Content. Your value is measured by how sharp, strategically grounded, and culturally relevant the team’s output becomes because of your influence.

The ideal candidate thinks in systems, not just posts. They bring deep platform fluency, trend forecasting discipline, competitive rigor, and strategic instinct to know when a creative concept is right for the moment, right for the brand, and right for the audience, or when it needs to be killed, redirected, or elevated. While Feastables is the highest-volume brand in the portfolio, this role requires shifting strategy across distinctly different product categories and audiences.

What You'll Do
Develop social media strategies for tentpole brand moments, seasonal pushes, and product launches, defining the strategic narrative, platform approach, and content mix before creative execution begins
Lead ongoing trend research across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, X/Twitter, and emerging platforms, surfacing opportunities, relevant formats, sounds, and cultural moments to the creative team with context on timing and brand fit
Work closely with the creative team to filter, refine, and pressure-test content concepts, bringing strategic insight, audience understanding, and competitive context to ensure the strongest ideas move forward
Build and maintain a competitive intelligence practice: monthly audits across each brand’s category analyzing what competitors are winning attention on social, what’s falling flat, and where Beast brands have whitespace to own
Track how brands (gaming, fashion, entertainment) engage Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences and translate relevant tactics into Beast brand-applicable strategies
Pull and analyze social analytics from Emplifi and platform dashboards weekly and monthly, translating performance data into clear insights that inform creative direction, content mix, and platform prioritization
Write detailed MrBeast channel integration briefs including talking points, product highlights, brand expectations, and retailer callouts aligned to each brand’s comms calendar
Build creative decks and strategic presentations for campaign pitches, strategy updates, and cross-functional alignment
Collaborate with the Creator Partnerships Manager to evaluate creator-brand fit and ensure partnership activations align with brand strategy and audience targets
Define and refine how each Beast brand should show up on each platform, including voice, format preferences, posting cadence, and content mix, and evolve that positioning as platforms and audiences shift
Contribute strategic input to social copy and creative briefs, ensuring platform-specific language drives conversation and shareability rather than defaulting to marketing language
Maintain a reference library of high-performing content examples from competitors, creators, and adjacent categories for team-wide learning and creative team inspiration
Adapt and shift between brand voices, tones, and audience contexts quickly, recognizing that what works for Feastables may not work for Apparel or Lunchly, and vice versa
What We're Looking For
3-5 years of experience in social media strategy, content strategy, or brand strategy for consumer-facing brands
Demonstrated ability to translate trend research and competitive analysis into actionable creative direction
Strong analytical skills with experience pulling insights from social analytics platforms (Emplifi, Sprout, or similar) and translating data into strategic recommendations
Proven ability to write compelling strategic briefs and campaign frameworks that give creative teams clear direction without being prescriptive
